name: Boy Bands
description: Pop boy bands from the '80s, '90s, 2000s, and beyond

questions.q: How many members are in the Backstreet Boys?

source.notes: AJ, Howie, Nick, Kevin, and Brian.

questions.q: The pop-rock boy band '5 Seconds of Summer' hail from which country?

source.notes: Formed in Sydney in 2011.

questions.q: Who was the lead singer of The Jackson 5?

source.notes: Later launched perhaps the biggest solo career in pop history.

questions.q: Which '*NSYNC' member went on to a hugely successful solo career after the group went on hiatus in 2002?

source.notes: Two Grammy-winning solo albums ('Justified', 'FutureSex/LoveSounds').

questions.q: Which Bradford-born singer was a member of One Direction until leaving the group in 2015?

source.notes: Departed to 'live as a normal 22-year-old'.

questions.q: Which UK boy band had Gary Barlow and Robbie Williams as members?

source.notes: Formed in Manchester in 1990.

questions.q: The Backstreet Boys formed in which U.S. city in 1993?

source.notes: Produced by Florida-based Trans Continental Records.

questions.q: Nick Lachey of 98 Degrees appeared in which MTV reality show with his then-wife Jessica Simpson?

source.notes: Ran 2003–2005 on MTV.

questions.q: The abbreviation 'NKOTB' stands for which boy band's name?

source.notes: Formed in Boston in 1984.

questions.q: Which English boy band, based in Liverpool, had hits like 'Back Here' and 'Still on Your Side'?

source.notes: Active 1996–2003 and reunited 2018.

questions.q: Which British boy band was formed in 2009 and had a #1 hit with 'Glad You Came'?

source.notes: Active 2009–2014, reunited 2021.

questions.q: One Direction was formed on which TV talent show in 2010?

source.notes: Simon Cowell paired up five solo contestants into a group.

questions.q: Boyz II Men's lengthy 1992 hit 'End of the Road' was co-written by which hitmaker?

source.notes: Featured in the 'Boomerang' soundtrack.

questions.q: The youngest original New Kids On The Block member, known as 'the baby-faced one', is who?

source.notes: Joined at age 14 when the group formed.

questions.q: Which Irish boy band formed in 1993 included Ronan Keating and Stephen Gately?

source.notes: Dublin-based; hits include 'Words' and 'Love Me for a Reason'.

questions.q: The 2000 MTV reality show 'Making the Band' put together which boy band, known for 'Liquid Dreams'?

source.notes: Formed by Lou Pearlman on the first season of 'Making the Band'.

questions.q: Lou Pearlman also created which two huge '90s boy bands before his fraud conviction?
choices: Boyz II Men and New Edition

source.notes: Pearlman was jailed in 2008 for running a Ponzi scheme; died in 2016.

questions.q: Which pop-punk British five-piece, formed in 2003, had hits like 'Year 3000' and 'Five Colours in Her Hair'?

source.notes: Named for the Back to the Future character.

questions.q: Menudo, the long-running Latin boy band that included a young Ricky Martin, hails from which country?

source.notes: Formed 1977; known for rotating lineups once members reached 16.

questions.q: Which Jonas Brothers member is married to 'Game of Thrones' actress Sophie Turner (Sansa Stark)?

source.notes: Married 2019, separated 2023.

questions.q: Take That reunited in 2005 without Robbie Williams; their first #1 comeback single was titled what?

source.notes: From the 2006 album 'Beautiful World'; UK #1 for 4 weeks.

questions.q: Korean boy band BTS, the global K-pop phenomenon, consists of how many members?

source.notes: RM, Jin, Suga, J-Hope, Jimin, V, and Jungkook.
